title: Multi-tissue multi-omics integration reveals tissue-specific pathways, gene networks and drug candidates for type 1 diabetes
authors: Blencowe M, Saleem Z, Liu R, Wang M, Tseng IH, Wier J, Mutter S, Vaida F, Guo Y, Sandholm N, Ackeifi C, Kaufman DL, Yang X
abstract_summary: Multi-tissue multi-omics analysis integrating human GWAS data for T1D with tissue-specific regulatory data across immune cells, adipose tissue, and pancreatic islets. Identified known immune pathways (adaptive immune responses, cytokine-mediated inflammation) and novel signals (RIG-I/MDA5 interferon induction, melanogenesis, steroid hormone synthesis, iron transport after HLA removal). Key driver genes: FYN, TAP1, WAS, HLA-B/C/G, LCK, LCP2. Drug repositioning predictions generated using LINCS L1000 and PharmOmics platforms, validated against electronic medical records from OneFlorida+ Clinical Data Network. Funded by Breakthrough T1D (formerly JDRF).
action_items: Assess drug repositioning candidates identified by LINCS L1000/PharmOmics for overlap with our islet repurposing analysis
Cross-reference key driver genes (FYN, TAP1, LCK) with our existing network models
Check if Breakthrough T1D co-author (Ackeifi C) indicates foundation interest in this direction
